howItWorks.intro: This cake pan size converter uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Round Pan Area
rules.explanation: The area of a circular pan based on its diameter.

rules.name: Scale Factor
rules.formula: Scale = New Pan Area / Original Pan Area
rules.explanation: Multiply all ingredient quantities by this factor when changing pan sizes.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: Can I use a 9-inch pan instead of an 8-inch?
faq.a: Yes, but a 9-inch round pan has about 27% more area than an 8-inch. Your cake will be thinner, so reduce baking time by 5-10 minutes and check early. You can also multiply the recipe by 1.27 to maintain the same thickness.

faq.q: How do I convert round to square pans?
faq.a: A round pan has less area than a square pan of the same dimension. A 9-inch round has roughly the same area as an 8-inch square. Use the area calculation to find the exact scaling factor for your specific sizes.
